_comment: Shared output-interpretation contract for the two grading runners. The INPUT fields (stdout/stderr/exitCode) are fed to RunnerCore's interpretScriptOutput, which BOTH runners now use (the worker directly; the browser via the wasm executeSuites bridge). `native` records the exact display strings interpretScriptOutput produces and is the single source of truth — Tests/WorkerTests/OutputContractTests.swift asserts the native build matches it, and Tests/BrowserRunnerJSTests/output-contract.test.mjs drives the REAL vendored wasm and asserts it matches too, so the two runners are byte-identical (status, shortResult, longResult). interpretScriptOutput strips a leading `<test>: ` label from shortResult (when the footer carries a `test` field) and surfaces a footer `traceback` field as longResult.

cases.name: fail with human message then JSON footer
error with footer traceback field (surfaced as longResult)
cases.stdout:
Could not test calculate_bmi
{"shortResult":"Q1: Could not test calculate_bmi","status":"error","test":"Q1","error":"Could not test calculate_bmi","traceback":"Traceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"t.py\", line 3\nNotImplementedError"}

native.longResult:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "t.py", line 3
NotImplementedError

cases.name: exit code 3 maps to fail (Marmoset convention), stderr only
JSON footer followed by trailing blank lines (last-non-empty-line robustness)
pass with human stdout then JSON footer (footer stripped on the pass path)
timeout wins over exit code (SIGKILL after the limit, empty output)
timeout with partial stderr surfaced (exit -1 SIGKILL)
